$comment: Example 1: Minimal PlanSpec — a simple documentation update

intent.goal_statement: Add installation instructions to the project README
intent.motivation: New users report they cannot figure out how to install the package
intent.success_definition: README contains a working installation section that new users can follow successfully
intent.scope_context: Single markdown file in a TypeScript npm package repository

out_of_scope: Changing the package name or publish config
assumptions: The package is published to npm under its current name
Node.js 18+ is the minimum supported runtime

approach.overview: Insert a standard 'Installation' section into README.md after the project description, before 'Usage'.
approach.rationale: Standard npm packages conventionally place installation instructions near the top of the README for discoverability.

alternatives_considered.rejection_reason: Adds an extra click for users; npm convention favors inline README sections

steps.title: Write installation section
steps.description: Add an '## Installation' heading with npm and yarn install commands, plus a note about Node.js version requirements.
steps.rationale: Covers the two most common package managers
steps.acceptance_criteria: Installation section is present, commands are syntactically correct, and running them in a clean environment installs the package

constraints.description: No breaking changes to existing README content
constraints.validation_method: Diff review shows only additions, no modifications to existing sections

success_criteria.description: A new user following the README instructions can install the package successfully
Installation section is within the first 50 lines of README
